title: Blocked-claim output

commands.summary: Final S16 closeout gate rerun after evidence creation

notes: No real client, matter, document, billing, settlement, credential, or secret data was used.

All commands are local validation, parser, test, Claude read-only review, or git whitespace checks; none execute service logic, create runtime routes, run Hermes validation as product runtime behavior, create Hermes evidence as product runtime behavior, mutate tenant lifecycle state, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write blocked claim outputs, write blocked claim records, notify blocked claim owners, mutate blocked claim refs, persist idempotency records, acquire locks, write databases, write storage, persist product state, map runtime errors, translate runtime exceptions, write error records, invoke Claude runtime review, write review queues, assign reviewers, notify reviewers, write human approval records, write approval queues, assign approvers, notify approvers, write approval records, create UI, replace human approval, or mutate product state.
Pre-closeout npm run rp00:control-plane:validate failed only because S16 evidence files were intentionally not created yet; this command is rerun after evidence creation as the final gate.
Claude C00 valid review completed with GO and no P0/P1/P2 findings. P3 notes are informational and require no code change.

evidence_summary.claude_review_dependency: C00 actual claude-opus-4-8 max read-only review completed once before production_ready
evidence_summary.human_approval_boundary: Human approval is not required for this synthetic metadata-only blocked-claim output boundary because no sensitive production mutation, client-facing output, external sharing, finance action, or real data operation occurs.
evidence_summary.blocked_claims: S16 emits canonical blocked claim refs and output entries as metadata only; it writes no blocked claim output, record, notification, UI, database, storage, audit event, Hermes evidence, queue item, or product state.
